title: Filter Window Functions Instantly via QUALIFY Clause
wizard_code:
-- 1-Pass Window Filtering (BigQuery, Snowflake)
SELECT id, dept, salary
FROM employees
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY dept ORDER BY salary DESC) = 1;
hinglish_explanation: Window functions ko filter karne ke liye external nested subqueries likhne ki zarurat nahi. `QUALIFY` clause select queries ke baad inline window outputs ko filter kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Explain SQL QUALIFY clause vs WHERE clause execution order in modern data warehouses.

title: Access Adjacent Row Values with LEAD() and LAG()
naive_code:
-- Heavy self-joins with offset coordinates to find day-over-day change
SELECT a.date, a.val - b.val
FROM sales a
JOIN sales b ON a.date = b.date + 1;
wizard_code:
-- Direct Offset Value Fetching
SELECT date, val,
       val - LAG(val, 1, 0) OVER (ORDER BY date) as daily_change
FROM sales;
hinglish_explanation: Pichhli row (LAG) ya Agli row (LEAD) ki value access karne ke liye self-joins mat likho. Window offsets index standard optimization parameters hain!
ai_prompt: Show how to use SQL LEAD() and LAG() functions with default fallback parameter values.

title: Division by Zero Protection via COALESCE and NULLIF
naive_code:
-- Crashes query with division by zero error when count is 0
SELECT total_sales / count_orders FROM store_metrics;
wizard_code:
-- Safe NULL-Fallback Division
SELECT COALESCE(total_sales / NULLIF(count_orders, 0), 0) FROM store_metrics;
hinglish_explanation: Order counts zero hone par query crash ho jaati hai. `NULLIF(col, 0)` zero ko NULL bana deta hai, aur division null ho kar `COALESCE` use baseline zero me return kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate SQL NULLIF and COALESCE functions for safe numeric calculations.

title: Count Conditional Segment Records via COUNT(CASE WHEN)
wizard_code:
-- 1-Pass Multi-Conditional Pivot Counts
SELECT
  COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'Active' THEN 1 END) as active_count,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN status = 'Inactive' THEN 1 END) as inactive_count
FROM users;
hinglish_explanation: Alag-alag metric counts ke liye multiple queries mat run karo. `COUNT(CASE WHEN cond THEN 1 END)` ek hi pass me pivoted counts calculate karta hai!
ai_prompt: Show SQL COUNT(CASE WHEN) pivot syntax vs SUM(CASE WHEN) pivot variations.

title: First and Last Values in Window with FIRST_VALUE()
naive_code:
-- Complex self-joins matching min timestamps manually
SELECT a.*, b.val as first_val
FROM data a
JOIN (SELECT id, val FROM data ORDER BY time LIMIT 1) b ON a.id = b.id;
wizard_code:
-- Native First Value Window function
SELECT id, val, time,
       FIRST_VALUE(val) OVER (PARTITION BY id ORDER BY time) as first_session_val
FROM sessions;
hinglish_explanation: Group partitions me chronological first ya last value extract karne ke liye self-joins ki jagah `FIRST_VALUE()` window function use karo!
ai_prompt: Explain window frames default specification and why LAST_VALUE needs ROWS BETWEEN.

title: Split Dataset into Equal Quantiles via NTILE(N)
naive_code:
-- Manual index sorting calculations to identify percentiles
-- ... complex calculations
wizard_code:
-- Divide Users into 4 Equal Spending Buckets (Quartiles)
SELECT id, spend,
       NTILE(4) OVER (ORDER BY spend DESC) as spend_quartile
FROM customer_spending;
hinglish_explanation: Customers ko spending capacity ke according 4 equal groups (quartiles) me bin karne ke liye simple `NTILE(4)` divide execution chalao!
ai_prompt: Show SQL NTILE() usage for dividing users into deciles (NTILE(10)).

title: Truncate Timestamps to Cohort Intervals via DATE_TRUNC()
naive_code:
-- Complex string casting and regex manipulation to strip month details
SELECT CAST(SUBSTR(CAST(created_at AS STRING), 1, 7) || '-01' AS DATE) FROM logs;
wizard_code:
-- Native Month Truncation
SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', created_at) as cohort_month FROM logs;
hinglish_explanation: Daily records timestamps ko cohort groups (Month level) alignment ke liye complex regex ki jagah direct `DATE_TRUNC('month', timestamp)` call karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate DATE_TRUNC parameters for weekly, monthly, and quarterly aggregations.

title: Evaluate Row-by-Row Array Functions via LATERAL JOIN
naive_code:
-- Iterating cursor queries row-wise inside store procedures
-- ... cursors loops
wizard_code:
-- Vectorized Row-by-Row Array Flattening (PostgreSQL/Snowflake)
SELECT u.name, t.tag
FROM users u
CROSS JOIN LATERAL UNNEST(u.tags_array) as t(tag);
hinglish_explanation: JSON columns ya array types me stored entries ko row data ke saath dynamic cross correlation logic match `LATERAL JOIN` se fast expand karo!
ai_prompt: Show how LATERAL JOIN (or CROSS APPLY) evaluates row-dependent functions dynamically.

title: Hierarchy Traversal with CTEs via WITH RECURSIVE
naive_code:
-- Multiple loops or hardcoded child joins limits
SELECT * FROM org a JOIN org b ON a.id = b.manager_id JOIN org c ON b.id = c.manager_id;
wizard_code:
-- Recursion CTE hierarchy tracker
WITH RECURSIVE org_hierarchy AS (
  SELECT id, manager_id, name, 1 as level FROM employees WHERE manager_id IS NULL
  UNION ALL
  SELECT e.id, e.manager_id, e.name, h.level + 1 FROM employees e
  JOIN org_hierarchy h ON e.manager_id = h.id
) SELECT * FROM org_hierarchy;
hinglish_explanation: Manager-Employee organizational tree hierarchy ya path traversal solve karne ke liye `WITH RECURSIVE` CTE query execute karo!
ai_prompt: Explain WITH RECURSIVE anchor query and recursive term execution loop.

title: Multi-Level Aggregates Pivot via GROUPING SETS
naive_code:
-- Running multiple aggregations and merging UNION ALL results
SELECT region, NULL, sum(val) FROM sales GROUP BY region
UNION ALL
SELECT NULL, year, sum(val) FROM sales GROUP BY year;
wizard_code:
-- 1-Pass Multi-Level Aggregations
SELECT region, year, sum(sales_val) as total
FROM sales
GROUP BY GROUPING SETS ((region), (year), ());
hinglish_explanation: Alag-alag group dimensions (Region totals, Annual totals) ke liye queries stacked union all mat chalao. `GROUPING SETS` single pass me perform kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Compare SQL GROUPING SETS vs CUBE() vs ROLLUP() subtotal aggregations.

title: Inline Aggregate Filtering via FILTER (WHERE ...)
naive_code:
-- CASE WHEN pivot sums inside aggregation parameters
SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN age > 30 THEN revenue ELSE 0 END) as senior_revenue FROM users;
wizard_code:
-- Standard Clean Filter Aggregate (PostgreSQL/SQL:2003)
SELECT SUM(revenue) FILTER (WHERE age > 30) as senior_revenue FROM users;
hinglish_explanation: Aggregation calculations me logic clean readable filter set rules apply check parameters, `FILTER (WHERE ...)` notation standard utilize karo!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate FILTER (WHERE) aggregate parameters in complex analytics reports.

title: SARGable Search queries for index utilization
naive_code:
-- Non-SARGable query (Function on index column invalidates index search)
SELECT id FROM logs WHERE DATE(created_at) = '2025-01-01';
wizard_code:
-- SARGable search range condition utilizing index lookup
SELECT id FROM logs WHERE created_at >= '2025-01-01 00:00:00' AND created_at < '2025-01-02 00:00:00';
hinglish_explanation: WHERE filters column index par nested functions (`DATE(col)`) index scans crash kar dete hain. Hamesha straight ranges (`col >= range_start`) pass karo!
ai_prompt: Explain SARGable queries rules and why string functions on indexes degrade SQL performance.

title: Gaps and Islands Sequential Groups classification
naive_code:
-- Loops tracking consecutive dates matches manually
-- ... cursor functions
wizard_code:
-- Row Number Difference groupings island locator
WITH ranked_logs AS (
  SELECT user_id, active_date,
         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY active_date) as rn,
         active_date - ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY active_date) as island_id
  FROM logins
) SELECT user_id, min(active_date), max(active_date), count(*) as consecutive_days
FROM ranked_logs GROUP BY user_id, island_id;
hinglish_explanation: User activity timeline me 'consecutive days active' streaks (Islands of continuous records) compute karne ke liye index offset differences utilize karo!
ai_prompt: Explain the Gaps-and-Islands algorithm mechanics in SQL database engines.

title: Deduplicate Records via ROW_NUMBER() OVER
naive_code:
-- Attempting joins with group min values to delete duplicates
DELETE FROM log WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT MIN(id) FROM log GROUP BY val);
hinglish_explanation: Duplicate user profiles me se latest profile retain coordinates group indicators logic `ROW_NUMBER() OVER` partitions clean trace deta hai!
ai_prompt: Show how to use ROW_NUMBER() vs RANK() for deduplication pipelines.

title: CUBE Subtotals aggregations multi dimension summary
naive_code:
-- Multiple groupings union all loops manually
SELECT a, b, sum(v) FROM t GROUP BY a, b UNION SELECT a, NULL, sum(v)...
wizard_code:
-- 1-Pass Comprehensive combinations subtotal grid
SELECT category, region, sum(sales_val) as total
FROM sales
GROUP BY CUBE (category, region);
hinglish_explanation: Dynamic aggregate options dimensions grid matrix calculations combinations `CUBE` single query pass me compute kar deta hai!
ai_prompt: Compare CUBE vs ROLLUP logic options in database subtotal configurations.

title: Calculate Cumulative relative ranks via PERCENT_RANK()
naive_code:
-- Manual count math variables calculations inside subqueries
SELECT score, (SELECT count(*) FROM t t2 WHERE t2.score <= t1.score)/total...
wizard_code:
-- Vectorized percent rank locator
SELECT name, score,
       PERCENT_RANK() OVER (ORDER BY score) * 100 as percentile_rank
FROM students;
hinglish_explanation: Distributions percentages scoring analytics parameters `PERCENT_RANK()` directly computes!
ai_prompt: Compare PERCENT_RANK() vs CUME_DIST() definitions.

title: Merge rows string items via STRING_AGG()
naive_code:
-- Cursors processing strings appends loops
-- ... cursor updates
wizard_code:
-- Native Group string concat aggregator
SELECT dept,
       STRING_AGG(employee_name, ', ' ORDER BY employee_name) as team_list
FROM employees GROUP BY dept;
hinglish_explanation: Group keys base elements flat comma text formats aggregations `STRING_AGG` (or `GROUP_CONCAT`) 1-liner evaluates!
ai_prompt: Show STRING_AGG variations for PostgreSQL, MySQL, and SQL Server.

title: Extract JSON keys fields JSON_EXTRACT()
naive_code:
-- String index matching offsets substrings splits manually
SELECT SUBSTR(data, INSTR(data, 'email') + 8, 10) FROM payloads;
hinglish_explanation: Semi-structured column payloads key variables extraction schema mapping `JSON_VALUE`/`JSON_EXTRACT` syntax handles!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate JSON parsing functions across Snowflake, BigQuery, and PostgreSQL.

title: Split Text fields by index position SPLIT_PART()
naive_code:
-- Complex character index subtraction string splits
SELECT SUBSTRING(email FROM 1 FOR POSITION('@' IN email)-1) FROM users;
wizard_code:
-- Native delimiter index split extractor
SELECT SPLIT_PART(email, '@', 1) as username FROM users;
hinglish_explanation: Delimiter string segments parsing coordinates (`email` domain splits) `SPLIT_PART(col, '@', 1)` simplifies!
ai_prompt: Show SPLIT_PART usage variations in data preparation.

title: Subqueries checks optimization EXISTS vs IN
naive_code:
-- Inefficient scanning loading full list mapping memory comparisons
SELECT name FROM users WHERE id IN (SELECT user_id FROM logs);
hinglish_explanation: Large datasets indices validity checks subqueries matching execution speedups `EXISTS` short-circuit lookup utilizes!
ai_prompt: Explain database planner optimizations differences of EXISTS vs IN.

title: Avoid sorting sorting duplicates UNION ALL vs UNION
naive_code:
-- Default UNION sorting full stacked records to purge duplicates (slow)
SELECT id FROM active UNION SELECT id FROM archive;
wizard_code:
-- Zero sorting concatenation stack
SELECT id FROM active UNION ALL SELECT id FROM archive;
hinglish_explanation: Stacking tables without duplicates purge check requirements UNION ALL zero sorting performance optimizations utilize runs!
ai_prompt: Compare UNION execution plan metrics vs UNION ALL execution metrics.

title: Upsert metadata tables via MERGE INTO
naive_code:
-- Two separate insert and update calls checking states manually
-- ... manual statements
wizard_code:
-- Unified Upsert transaction statement
MERGE INTO users t USING source_table s ON t.id = s.id
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET t.email = s.email
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (id, email) VALUES (s.id, s.email);
hinglish_explanation: Data pipeline table updates insert and updates dual operations synchronization `MERGE INTO` unified execution handles!
ai_prompt: Show MERGE INTO usage specifications for sync databases tables.

title: Running average windows specify frames frame bounds
naive_code:
-- Manual self join date offsets loops calculating averages
# ... manual calculations
wizard_code:
-- 3-Days dynamic moving window frame sum
SELECT date, val,
       SUM(val) OVER (ORDER BY date ROWS BETWEEN 2 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW) as rolling_sum_3d
FROM daily_metrics;
hinglish_explanation: Moving window calculations coordinate boundary bounds window frame limits `ROWS BETWEEN` specifies!
ai_prompt: Explain ROWS vs RANGE parameter constraints in SQL window functions.

title: Median distributions calculation PERCENTILE_CONT
naive_code:
-- Row number sorting offset calculations manually
# ... manual calculations
wizard_code:
-- Direct exact median target value query
SELECT dept,
       PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY salary) OVER (PARTITION BY dept) as median_salary
FROM employees;
hinglish_explanation: Group boundaries distributions exact median outputs statistics parameters calculations `PERCENTILE_CONT` evaluates!
ai_prompt: Explain difference between PERCENTILE_CONT (continuous interpolation) vs PERCENTILE_DISC (discrete selection).

title: Custom sorting prioritizations CASE WHEN ORDER BY
naive_code:
-- Alphabetical ordering sorting categories arbitrary layout outputs
SELECT name, role FROM users ORDER BY role;
wizard_code:
-- Custom order weight index mapping
SELECT name, role FROM users ORDER BY
  CASE WHEN role = 'CEO' THEN 1
       WHEN role = 'Manager' THEN 2
       ELSE 3 END ASC;
hinglish_explanation: Categorical strings column output order sorting prioritizations customizations `CASE WHEN` inline ORDER BY handles!
ai_prompt: Show order by case when variations for sorting metrics priorities.

title: Flatten structured JSON arrays via UNNEST()
naive_code:
-- Slicing array positions manually
SELECT tags[1], tags[2] FROM items;
hinglish_explanation: JSON columns tag arrays cells row by row expansion unnesting `UNNEST()` simplifies!
ai_prompt: Show UNNEST vs LATERAL FLATTEN implementations across different databases.

title: Dynamic dates gaps generator recursive dates table
naive_code:
-- Left joining timeline transaction data missing dates drop silently
SELECT date, count(*) FROM logs GROUP BY date;
wizard_code:
-- Complete sequential dates calendar generator
WITH RECURSIVE calendar AS (
  SELECT DATE('2025-01-01') as date_val
  UNION ALL
  SELECT date_val + 1 FROM calendar WHERE date_val < DATE('2025-01-10')
) SELECT c.date_val, count(l.id) FROM calendar c
LEFT JOIN logs l ON c.date_val = DATE(l.created_at) GROUP BY c.date_val;
hinglish_explanation: TimeSeries gaps analysis calendar framework sequence values generation `WITH RECURSIVE` timeline creates!
ai_prompt: Demonstrate date generating techniques using GENERATE_SERIES() in PostgreSQL vs recursive CTEs.

title: Null values sorting priorities NULLS LAST
naive_code:
-- Null values appearing at the top when sorting descending (distorts analytics dashboards)
SELECT score FROM leaderboard ORDER BY score DESC;
wizard_code:
-- Clear null values placement constraint
SELECT score FROM leaderboard ORDER BY score DESC NULLS LAST;
hinglish_explanation: Leaderboard score metric listings sorting null entries bottom priorities alignments `NULLS LAST` evaluates!
ai_prompt: Show database query options for NULLS FIRST and NULLS LAST sorting overrides.

title: Calculate Epoch timestamps conversions extraction
naive_code:
-- Manual string parses calculating datetime diff values
# ... manual calculations
hinglish_explanation: Timeline duration intervals unix epoch seconds conversions `EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM ...)` simplifies!
ai_prompt: Show timestamp conversion to unix seconds across BigQuery and PostgreSQL.

title: Dynamic Pivot aggregates using CASE WHEN
naive_code:
-- Complex GROUP BY loops sorting rows horizontally
# ... manual queries
wizard_code:
-- Vertical rows to Horizontal columns pivots
SELECT year,
       SUM(CASE WHEN quarter = 'Q1' THEN revenue END) as q1_rev,
       SUM(CASE WHEN quarter = 'Q2' THEN revenue END) as q2_rev
FROM sales GROUP BY year;
hinglish_explanation: Sales records metrics category columns vertical rows horizontal expansion pivots `SUM(CASE WHEN)` handles!
ai_prompt: Show PIVOT syntax variations in Snowflake vs CASE WHEN pivot methods.

title: Exclude columns indexing Covering Index
naive_code:
-- Creating simple index that still requires database heap lookup for selection fields
CREATE INDEX idx_name ON users(email);
wizard_code:
-- Covering index including output selections fields directly
CREATE INDEX idx_name ON users(email) INCLUDE (user_id, status);
hinglish_explanation: Database search performance queries heap lookup table trips avoid coordinates index `INCLUDE` properties sets!
ai_prompt: Explain database covering index mechanics and why INCLUDE columns speed up SELECT queries.

title: CTE reuse efficiency Materialized CTEs
naive_code:
-- Default CTE query rerun multiple times inside execution plan (slow)
WITH heavy_step AS (SELECT * FROM log) SELECT ...
hinglish_explanation: Complex subquery groups execution plan calculations cache optimizations parameter `MATERIALIZED` CTE set runs!
ai_prompt: Explain CTE inline expansion vs materialization optimization strategies.

title: Non Equi Joins range associations
naive_code:
-- Mapping coordinates values loops checking ranges
# ... manual loops
wizard_code:
-- Join matching bounds condition
SELECT e.name, j.grade
FROM employees e
JOIN job_grades j ON e.salary BETWEEN j.min_salary AND j.max_salary;
hinglish_explanation: Equality variables matching range check boundaries mappings `BETWEEN` parameter non-equi join executes!
ai_prompt: Show non-equi join use cases in time interval data overlaps.

title: Self Join child parent hierarchy checks
naive_code:
-- Subqueries indexing managers keys manually
# ... complex queries
wizard_code:
-- Self JOIN manager mapping
SELECT e.name as employee, m.name as manager
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN employees m ON e.manager_id = m.id;
hinglish_explanation: Manager Employee connections relational structure mappings self JOIN checks!
ai_prompt: Show self-join applications in tree hierarchies tracking.

title: Partition Pruning query performance checks
naive_code:
-- Querying database partitioned table using non-key metrics (scans full cluster)
SELECT * FROM logs WHERE severity = 'ERROR';
wizard_code:
-- Query utilizing partition key to prune data scan size
SELECT * FROM logs WHERE created_date = '2025-01-01' AND severity = 'ERROR';
hinglish_explanation: Partitioned data tables query constraints execution partition key matching `created_date` constraints scans limits!
ai_prompt: Explain database partition pruning mechanics and how it reduces data query costs.

title: Case Insensitive String Matches via ILIKE
naive_code:
-- Lowercasing columns manually to match string patterns (degrades index usage)
SELECT * FROM users WHERE LOWER(email) LIKE '%gmail.com';
wizard_code:
-- Native Case-Insensitive Pattern Match
SELECT * FROM users WHERE email ILIKE '%gmail.com';
hinglish_explanation: Text categories pattern search case matching string calculations case insensitivity ILIKE directly runs!
ai_prompt: Compare SQL LIKE vs ILIKE vs REGEXP_LIKE performance.

title: Type Casting syntax conversions type
naive_code:
-- Verbose standard SQL CAST function syntax
SELECT CAST(amount AS INT) FROM sales;
wizard_code:
-- Clean Double Colon Type Casting (PostgreSQL/Redshift)
SELECT amount::INT FROM sales;
hinglish_explanation: Type conversions castings data mappings double colon `::INT` syntax shorthand clean execution gives!
ai_prompt: Show SQL data type casting variations and conversion error handling methods.

title: Explain Plan execution pipeline analysis
naive_code:
-- Running query blindly without verifying indices scans
SELECT * FROM user_scores;
hinglish_explanation: Database planner execution steps analysis index check scanning warnings query profiling EXPLAIN plans evaluates!
ai_prompt: Show how to read EXPLAIN execution plans outputs for tracing slow query scans.

title: Limit Offset Pagination optimizations limit
naive_code:
-- High offset page pagination scans millions rows slow executions
SELECT * FROM logs ORDER BY id LIMIT 10 OFFSET 1000000;
wizard_code:
-- Keyset pagination keyset range constraint
SELECT * FROM logs WHERE id > 1000000 ORDER BY id LIMIT 10;
hinglish_explanation: High page offsets data queries database scans load reduction range checks coordinates keyset paginator!
ai_prompt: Compare offset-based pagination vs keyset pagination (seek method) in SQL database performance.

title: Database transaction isolation levels concurrency

title: Division by Zero threshold protection NULLIF

title: Upsert table configurations MERGE INTO
naive_code:
-- Dual insert and updates statements manually executed
# ... manual updates
wizard_code:
-- Unified Upsert transaction statement
MERGE INTO target t USING source s ON t.id = s.id
WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET t.val = s.val
WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (id, val) VALUES (s.id, s.val);
hinglish_explanation: Data pipeline table updates insert and updates dual operations synchronization `MERGE INTO` unified execution handles!
ai_prompt: Show MERGE INTO usage specifications for sync databases tables.
